title: The Silent Stumble in the Study
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art curator, was found dead in the cramped study with a lone shoe, a remote control, a water bottle and a backpack. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Harlow
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Trevor Finch helped me move the shoe aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Miriam Clarke and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Trevor Finch and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Miriam Clarke
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the remote control.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Trevor Finch
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, I helped Gareth Harlow move the shoe aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Harlow and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitcombe's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Shoe
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the shoe is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Harlow and Trevor Finch both state that they moved the shoe aside at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Miriam Clarke's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Remote Control
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged in a narrow edge of the remote control on top of a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Miriam Clarke has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Miriam Clarke killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement scheme.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitcombe drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Miriam Clarke claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Gareth Harlow and Trevor Finch left together at 8:45 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the water bottle and the black and gold fragment recovered from the remote control place Miriam Clarke inside afterwards. Miriam Clarke therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the water bottle during the murder window.
